Chapter 1 Cell Structure
Cells are the basic structural and functional units of life. The cell membrane controls movement of substances into and out of the cell. The nucleus stores genetic material and coordinates cell activity. Mitochondria release usable energy through cellular respiration. Ribosomes synthesize proteins.

Chapter 2 Photosynthesis
Photosynthesis converts light energy into chemical energy. Chlorophyll absorbs light in chloroplasts. Plants use carbon dioxide and water to produce glucose and oxygen. Light intensity, carbon dioxide concentration, and temperature can affect the rate of photosynthesis.

Chapter 3 Human Respiration
The respiratory system exchanges oxygen and carbon dioxide. Air travels through the trachea and bronchi to the lungs. Alveoli provide a large, thin, moist surface for gas exchange. Oxygen diffuses into blood while carbon dioxide diffuses into the alveoli.
